prompt: Which cookie weakness is shown here?
code_snippet:
Set-Cookie: session=...; Secure; HttpOnly
(no SameSite attribute set)

options.text: Sensitive Cookie with Improper SameSite Attribute

explanation: The `session` cookie sets Secure and HttpOnly but omits SameSite, so the browser attaches it to cross-site requests — which makes CSRF easier — Sensitive Cookie with Improper SameSite Attribute (CWE-1275). CSRF (CWE-352) is the *attack* this enables; the cookie misconfiguration itself is CWE-1275. Fix: set SameSite=Lax or Strict.
